message:
test: harden conformance harness tests against fork-deadlock and shared-output races

Three independent test-only fixes that surfaced while wiring up the host_resolve refactor's verification path. None change product behavior.

* err_abi_test.rs::err_set_exit_hook_runs_before_errx_in_child — stop forking from inside the multi-threaded `cargo test` harness. Forking a process that holds Rust mutexes (e.g. allocator, pipeline init, errno cache) can deadlock the child if any other test thread owned that state at the moment of fork. Reshape the test to spawn a fresh test-binary child via `Command::new(current_exe)` running an `#[ignore]`-gated `err_set_exit_child_process` test that installs the hook and calls `errx(7)`. The parent now asserts on `output.status.code() == Some(7)` and on the eval marker + diagnostic appearing in the child's stderr instead of plumbing a pipe through `fork`.

* claim_reconciliation_test.rs — extract a `parse_report(stdout, stderr)` helper that turns the four duplicated `from_str(...).unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(...))` blocks into one call site, so a parse failure prints exactly one diagnostic with both stdout and stderr. Also bump the README-drift test's marker from `total_exported=3996` → `total_exported=4109` so the substitution lands now that the latest NIS/xdr porting commits raised the classified surface.

* per_symbol_fixture_tests_test.rs — `per_symbol_report_generates_successfully` and `per_symbol_report_reproducible` were both writing to the *checked-in* `tests/conformance/per_symbol_fixture_tests.v1.json`, which races every other test in the workspace that reads that file (fixture coverage, claim reconciliation, etc.) and produces flaky-test failures when two cargo-test threads scheduled the generator concurrently. Switch to `temp_report_path("<label>")` — `${TMPDIR}/frankenlibc-per-symbol-<label>-<pid>-<nanos>.json` — so each invocation owns its own output. The reproducibility test now generates twice into separate temp paths and compares hashes.

* stub_guard_test.rs::abi_source_mentions_export — recognise the bare-arg form `!(symbol);` (no parens, no value) as a valid export macro invocation. Previously the matcher only saw `!(symbol(...)` and `!(symbol, ...)`, so symbols exported via the no-arg macro variant were flagged as missing-from-source. No new test added — this is a pure recognizer fix that lets the existing guard pass on the latest export list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
